chunks.content:
Muhammad Umar Farooq - BS AI Student | Agentic AI Developer
Location: Park View City, Lahore, Pakistan
Contact: momerfarooq223@gmail.com
BS Artificial Intelligence student (6th semester) at the University of Management & Technology, Lahore, maintaining a 3.81 CGPA on a 70% merit scholarship with Dean's Award (top 10% department SGPA). Currently enrolled in the Panaversity Agentic AI Program, building production-grade autonomous agents using LLMs, Groq and multi-agent architectures. Experienced in computer vision, NLP, workflow automation (n8n) and full-stack ML pipelines with 13+ shipped projects including 6+ autonomous AI agents.

BS Artificial Intelligence from University of Management & Technology (UMT), Lahore (Oct 2023 — Present)
CGPA: 3.81/4.00
Awards: 70% Merit Scholarship awarded based on academic performance, Dean's Award — top 10% SGPA in the AI department

FSc Pre-Medical from Punjab Group of Colleges (PGC), Lahore (Sep 2021 — Jun 2023)
CGPA: N/A
Awards: 50% Merit Scholarship

Matric (Science) from Iqra Huffaz Secondary School, Lahore (Jun 2019 — Jun 2021)
CGPA: N/A
Awards: Biology (A+), Chemistry (A+), Physics (A+)

Academic Tutor (Peer Tutoring) at UMT, Lahore (Mar 2025 — Jul 2025)
Tutored peers across multiple AI and programming subjects, helping students improve grades and grasp complex concepts through adaptive teaching methods.
Responsibilities: Tutored peers across multiple AI and programming subjects, Helped students improve grades and grasp complex concepts, Adapted teaching methods to fit different learning styles

Customer Services — Shopify Store at Remote (Nov 2024 — Jan 2025)
Resolved customer inquiries, managed shipments, and coordinated with suppliers for a Shopify store.
Responsibilities: Responded to customer inquiries via email and chat, Resolved issues related to orders, returns and product information, Handled post-purchase support and tracked shipments

Taxi Dispatching & Limousine Services at Grasp Solutions (Pvt) Ltd, Lahore (Jun 2024 — Sep 2024)
Managed high-volume client interactions in taxi dispatch operations.
Responsibilities: Handled reservation entries using Limo Anywhere Software, Maintained high accuracy and speed in data entry under time constraints

Truck Dispatch (US Campaign) at SMZ Dispatch Services (Feb 2024 — Apr 2024)
Cross-cultural coordination with US-based truck drivers in the sales department.
Responsibilities: Managed communication with US-based truck drivers, Demonstrated reliability in maintaining schedules and providing logistical support

Skills:

ai_ml: LLM Integration (Groq, LLaMA), Agentic AI Architecture, Machine Learning, Computer Vision (OpenCV, YOLO), NLP & Text Classification, Scikit-learn, MCP (Model Context Protocol)
languages: Python (Advanced), C++, SQL, HTML/CSS/JavaScript
frameworks: FastAPI, Flask, Streamlit, Gradio
tools: n8n Workflow Automation, Git/GitHub, Docker, PyMuPDF, openpyxl, python-docx, Jupyter, VS Code, Playwright
data_engineering: Pandas, NumPy, MySQL, SQLite, Parquet
deep_learning: PyTorch, TensorFlow/Keras, DistilBERT, Transformers (Hugging Face)
spoken_languages: Urdu (Native), English (Advanced), Turkish (Basic)
interests: Competitive Programming (LeetCode), Open-source AI projects, Agentic AI development

Project: Personal AI Employee
Description: A fully autonomous Personal AI Employee that monitors your Gmail, reasons about incoming tasks, waits for your approval, and takes real-world actions — all while you stay in control. Built for the Panaversity Hackathon 0.
Problem Solved: Eliminates manual email triage and response drafting by creating an autonomous agent that monitors Gmail, reasons about incoming tasks using LLMs, generates contextual replies, and auto-posts LinkedIn content — all with human-in-the-loop approval to keep the user in control.
Key Features: Gmail auto-monitoring with launchd cron (every 2 minutes), AI reasoning loop using Claude Code + Groq LLaMA 3.3 70B, Human-in-the-loop (HITL) approval workflow via file system
Tech Stack: Python 3.13, Claude Code, Groq LLaMA 3.3 70B, Gmail API, Playwright
GitHub: https://github.com/omerfarooq223/AI_Employee_Vault

Project: AutoGrader Agent
Description: AI-powered grading agent that automates assignment evaluation using LLMs. Available as both a CLI tool and a Streamlit web UI. Accepts a ZIP of student submissions and an assignment brief, generates structured rubrics, grades each submission, detects plagiarism, and outputs styled Excel reports.
Problem Solved: Eliminates the tedious, time-consuming manual grading process for educators by automating rubric generation, per-criterion grading with LLMs, plagiarism detection (TF-IDF + n-gram), and producing color-coded Excel reports with class statistics and insights.
Key Features: Auto-generates structured grading rubrics via LLM (Groq/Gemini), Concurrent LLM-based grading with per-criterion scoring, Dual plagiarism detection (TF-IDF cosine + character n-gram Jaccard)
Tech Stack: Python, Groq (LLaMA 3.1 8B Instant), Gemini API (Vision fallback), Streamlit, PyMuPDF
GitHub: https://github.com/omerfarooq223/AutoGrader

Project: CareerPilot Agent
Description: A fully autonomous AI agent that watches your GitHub, tracks your skill gaps and coaches you toward landing your target internship, week over week. Features a premium glassmorphism web dashboard, floating chat widget, and 10 modular skills.
Problem Solved: Most students send applications without knowing where they stand. CareerPilot reads your GitHub, scores your profile against your target role, identifies specific skill gaps, and nudges you weekly to close them — turning vague career anxiety into structured, actionable coaching.
Key Features: Continuous agentic loop: Observe → Analyze → Remember → Plan → Act, 10 modular skills (audit_repo, suggest_project, rewrite_readme, dev_card, mock_interview_prep, weekly_nudge, linkedin_writer, update_goals, gap_analyzer, github_observer), Premium glassmorphism web dashboard with floating chat widget
Tech Stack: Python, FastAPI, Groq (LLaMA 3.3 70B), Pydantic, SQLite
GitHub: https://github.com/omerfarooq223/CareerPilot-Agent

Project: DSA C++ Core & Semester Archive
Description: A comprehensive collection of Data Structures and Algorithms (DSA) in C++, featuring both modernized, template-based implementations (C++17) and original university coursework from the 3rd semester.
Problem Solved: Provides a well-organized reference library of core DSA implementations in C++ — both as clean, generic template-based code for reuse and as an archived record of university coursework for study and revision.
Key Features: Generic programming with C++ templates (works with any data type), Memory-safe manual memory management with recursive destructors, BST, Linked List, Stack, Sorting, Searching, Array Operations
Tech Stack: C++17, g++, Make
GitHub: https://github.com/omerfarooq223/DSA_CPP

Project: UrduPlanner Agent
Description: AI-powered Urdu lesson planner that generates weekly lesson plans from textbook PDFs using LLMs. Extracts content via OCR, repairs garbled Urdu text, and fills a structured Word template via a web interface (and optional CLI).
Problem Solved: Automates the time-consuming process of creating weekly Urdu lesson plans for teachers. Handles PDF text extraction (including scanned pages via OCR), intelligently repairs garbled Urdu text using LLMs, generates structured lesson content, fixes RTL issues, and fills Word templates — producing ready-to-print planners.
Key Features: Concurrent processing — parallelizes PDF extraction, OCR repair, and LLM generation across 3 lessons, Robust page range parsing (e.g., '1, 3, 5-10, 15'), Live progress dashboard with Rich progress bars
Tech Stack: Python, Groq (LLaMA 3.3 70B), python-docx, PyMuPDF, pytesseract
GitHub: https://github.com/omerfarooq223/UrduPlanner

Project: YOLO Vehicle Detection
Description: A high-performance vehicle detection system using YOLOv10x for images and YOLOv8n for videos. Capable of detecting cars, trucks, buses, and motorbikes with high accuracy and speed.
Problem Solved: Provides an easy-to-use vehicle detection system that leverages state-of-the-art YOLO models for both static image analysis (high accuracy with YOLOv10x) and real-time video processing (high frame rate with YOLOv8n), with automated output management.
Key Features: Precise image detection using YOLOv10x, Fast video detection using YOLOv8n for high frame rate, Easy-to-use CLI (detect.py)
Tech Stack: Python, Ultralytics (YOLOv8/v10), PyTorch, torchvision, OpenCV
GitHub: https://github.com/omerfarooq223/YOLO_Vehicle_Detection

Project: AutoResearch Agent
Description: An autonomous AI research agent that takes any topic, breaks it into targeted sub-questions, searches the live web in parallel, verifies its own claims, and produces a professional research report — typically in 1-2 minutes.
Problem Solved: Unlike basic chat tools, AutoResearch provides a multi-step research pipeline that breaks topics into sub-questions, searches in parallel, self-verifies claims against sources, flags low-confidence statements, and generates downloadable branded reports (PDF/Word/HTML/Markdown) — designed for structured handoff rather than raw chat output.
Key Features: Breaks any topic into 5 targeted sub-questions automatically, Parallel web search across all sub-questions (~4x faster), Self-critique loop — flags unsupported or partially supported claims
Tech Stack: Python, Groq (LLaMA 3.3 70B), Tavily (Advanced Web Search), ReportLab (PDF generation), python-docx (Word generation)
GitHub: https://github.com/omerfarooq223/autoresearch-agent

Project: Course Registration System
Description: A terminal-based university course registration system written in C++, modernized for multi-student support and per-course waitlists. Demonstrates BST, Stack, Queue, and Linked List data structures in a practical application.
Problem Solved: Provides a practical demonstration of multiple data structures (BST, Stack, Queue, Linked List) working together in a real-world scenario — a university course registration system with student management, waitlists, auto-enrollment, and undo functionality.
Key Features: BST for department and student management, Per-course waitlists using Queue data structure, Auto-enrollment from waitlist when a student drops a course
Tech Stack: C++17, g++
GitHub: https://github.com/omerfarooq223/course-registration-system

Project: Language Recognition using Logistic Regression
Description: A machine learning project that classifies audio recordings into 4 languages — Urdu, English, Turkish, and Mixed — using MFCC features and Multinomial Logistic Regression implemented from scratch in NumPy.
Problem Solved: Demonstrates building a language recognition classifier from scratch using only NumPy — classifying self-recorded audio data into 4 languages using MFCC feature extraction, softmax, cross-entropy loss, and batch gradient descent with L2 regularization. Achieves ~80.77% accuracy.
Key Features: Multinomial Logistic Regression built from scratch using only NumPy, MFCC feature extraction (52-dimensional feature vectors), 4 language classification: Urdu, English, Turkish, Mixed
Tech Stack: Python, NumPy, librosa, scikit-learn, Matplotlib
GitHub: https://github.com/omerfarooq223/language-recognition-logistic-regression

Project: Multi-Modal AI + NLP + MLOps Pipeline
Description: End-to-end implementation of all four Project Nexus assessment challenges: Multi-Modal Inference Pipeline (OpenCV, MediaPipe, spaCy), Transformer-Driven Sentiment Analysis (DistilBERT vs Custom LSTM), Production-Grade MLOps Simulation (Docker, Cloud), and Data Engineering for Scale (Pandas, SQL indexing, Parquet).
Problem Solved: Provides a comprehensive, production-grade ML pipeline that combines computer vision (MobileNetV2 + OpenCV + MediaPipe), NLP (spaCy + NLTK VADER), transformer fine-tuning (DistilBERT vs custom LSTM comparison), Docker containerization, cloud deployment strategies, and a scalable data ingestion pipeline — all in a single project.
Key Features: Multi-modal inference: combined image classification + text analysis via FastAPI, MobileNetV2 ImageNet classifier + OpenCV/MediaPipe visual features, spaCy NLP + NLTK VADER sentiment analysis
Tech Stack: Python 3.11, FastAPI, uvicorn, gunicorn, OpenCV
GitHub: https://github.com/omerfarooq223/multimodal-mlops-pipeline

Project: n8n Jira-Slack Integration
Description: Automated workflows connecting Jira and Slack using n8n and OpenRouter AI. Includes Jira status updater, issue creator with AI parsing, and real-time sprint summary — all triggered via Slack slash commands.
Problem Solved: Streamlines project management by automating the Jira-Slack communication loop — letting teams update issue statuses, create new issues with AI-powered parsing, and get real-time sprint summaries directly from Slack slash commands without switching tools.
Key Features: Jira Status Updater via Slack slash command (/jira-update), Jira Issue Creator with AI parsing (/jira-create), Real-time Kanban board sprint summary (/sprint-summary)
Tech Stack: n8n, Jira Cloud API, Slack API, OpenRouter AI, ngrok
GitHub: https://github.com/omerfarooq223/n8n-jira-slack

Project: n8n Workflows Collection
Description: A collection of automation workflows built with n8n, OpenRouter AI, and Google Sheets. Includes GitHub API integration, webhook endpoints, custom code logic, and AI-powered customer support bots with automatic issue categorization.
Problem Solved: Provides a library of reusable n8n workflow templates for common automation tasks — from GitHub API data fetching to AI-powered customer support bots that categorize tickets and log conversations, reducing manual support workload.
Key Features: GitHub API Integration workflow, Webhook data response endpoint, Custom JavaScript code logic in n8n
Tech Stack: n8n, OpenRouter AI, Google Sheets API, JavaScript, REST APIs
GitHub: https://github.com/omerfarooq223/n8n-workflows

Project: Smart Parking Detection System
Description: AI-powered parking lot analysis system using fine-tuned YOLOv8 models to detect occupied and free parking spots from images and video. Features zone-based analysis, weighted spot scoring, entrance-aware recommendations, and an optional AI chatbot.
Problem Solved: Automates parking lot monitoring by using computer vision to detect occupied vs. free parking spots, score them based on multiple heuristics (confidence, distance to entrance, cluster bonus, spot size), and provide ranked recommendations — replacing manual lot surveillance.
Key Features: Fine-tuned YOLOv8 with optional ensemble mode, Image and video analysis support, Zone-based occupancy analysis (quadrant heat map)
Tech Stack: Python, Ultralytics (YOLOv8), OpenCV, NumPy, SciPy
GitHub: https://github.com/omerfarooq223/parking-detection-system

Project: Solana (SOL) Trading Agent Skills
Description: A suite of 3 custom Agent Skills for Claude Code to automate the repetitive cognitive tasks involved in spot cryptocurrency trading. Transforms manual monitoring and calculation workflows into a streamlined, agentic process.
Problem Solved: Eliminates tedious manual tasks in crypto trading — price monitoring (~10 min/day saved), position sizing and risk-to-reward calculations (~5 min/trade saved), and trade journaling (~5 min/trade saved) — by packaging them as reusable Claude Code agent skills.
Key Features: SOL Price Sentinel — instant market briefing via CoinGecko API, Risk Manager — 1% portfolio risk rule position sizing calculator, Trade Logger — automated CSV-based trade journaling with running balance
Tech Stack: Python, requests, CoinGecko API, Claude Code (Agent Skills), CSV
GitHub: https://github.com/omerfarooq223/solana_trading_agent
